2604.17718.title: Do LLMs Use Cultural Knowledge Without Being Told? A Multilingual Evaluation of Implicit Pragmatic Adaptation
2604.17718.paper_summary: Many benchmarks show that large language models can answer direct questions about culture. We study a different question: do they also change how they speak when culture is only implied by the situation? We evaluate 60 culturally grounded conversational scenarios across five languages in three conditions: a neutral baseline (Prompt A), an explicit cultural instruction (Prompt B), and implicit situational cueing (Prompt C). We score responses on 12 pragmatic features covering deference to authority, individual-versus-group framing, and uncertainty management. We define Pragmatic Context Sensitivity (PCS) as the fraction of the Prompt A->B shift that reappears under Prompt A->C. Across four deployed LLMs and five languages (English, German, Hindi, Nepali, Urdu), the primary stable-only PCS mean is 0.196 (SD = 0.113), indicating that the models recover only about one-fifth of the pragmatic shift they can produce when instructed explicitly. Transfer is strongest for authority-related cues (0.299) and weakest for individual-versus-group framing (0.120). Uncertainty-related behaviour is mixed: hedging density exhibits negative explicit gaps in all five languages, suggesting that alignment training actively suppresses the target behaviour. Because Hindi and Urdu share core grammar yet index distinct cultural communities, we use them as a natural control; a paired analysis finds no reliable baseline difference (t = 0.96, p = 0.339, dz = 0.06), suggesting that models respond primarily to linguistic structure rather than to the cultural associations a language carries. We argue that multilingual cultural pragmatics is an explicit-versus-implicit deployment problem, not only a factual knowledge problem.
2604.17718.paper_summary_zh: 許多基準顯示，大型語言模型能夠回答有關文化的直接問題。<lb/>我們研究一個不同的問題：當文化僅僅由情境暗示時，它們的語言表達是否也會改變？<lb/>我們在三種條件下評估了60個以文化為基礎的對話場景，涵蓋五種語言：中立基線（提示A）、明確的文化指示（提示B）和隱含的情境提示（提示C）。<lb/>我們對12個涵蓋對權威的尊重、個體與群體框架以及不確定性管理的實用特徵進行評分。<lb/>我們將實用情境敏感性（PCS）定義為在提示A到提示B的轉變中，重新出現於提示A到提示C的比例。<lb/>在四個部署的LLM和五種語言（英語、德語、印地語、尼泊爾語、烏爾都語）中，主要的穩定僅PCS均值為0.196（標準差=0.113），這表明模型在明確指示下僅能恢復約五分之一的實用轉變。<lb/>對於與權威相關的提示，轉移最強（0.299），而對於個體與群體框架則最弱（0.120）。<lb/>與不確定性相關的行為則是混合的：在所有五種語言中，模糊性密度顯示出負的明確差距，這表明對齊訓練積極抑制了目標行為。<lb/>由於印地語和烏爾都語共享核心語法，但指向不同的文化社群，我們將它們用作自然對照；配對分析發現沒有可靠的基線差異（t = 0.96，p = 0.339，dz = 0.06），這表明模型主要對語言結構作出反應，而不是對語言所承載的文化聯想作出反應。<lb/>我們認為，多語言文化實用學是一個明確與隱含的部署問題，而不僅僅是事實知識問題。
2604.17718.author: Mehwish Nasim et.al.
2604.17718.authors: Mehwish Nasim, Sanjeevan Selvaganapathy, Neel Ganapathi Sabhahit, Marie Griesbach, Pranav Bhandari, Janina Lütke Stockdiek, Lennart Schäpermeier, Usman Naseem, Christian Grimme
